brief_justification: Arrives at correct answer (No) with valid quantitative reasoning showing 66ms buffer dwarfs 13µs total uncertainty; traces second-order effects including worst-case range, margin analysis, and generalizes when clock uncertainty would matter; notes API timeout and proceeds with native reasoning but does not name biases or explicitly check assumptions mid-analysis; computes worst-case range and margin analysis from a different angle but skips boundary/counterfactual tests; acknowledges GPS correction direction and uncertainty range but lacks structured confidence-level separation; presents only one GPS correction interpretation without considering alternatives like whether the exchange already compensated for the delay; every calculation step is shown with intermediate derivations — fully reproducible; explicitly accounts for GPS delay and atomic clock precision rather than treating them as negligible, but does not name the avoided anti-pattern; avoids global simultaneity assumption and physics blindness, applies corrections rather than raw comparison, but does not explicitly flag any suppressed mode by name; follows identify-observers → extract-frames → apply-corrections → classify-within-window path from topology, but presents it linearly rather than showing branching gates explicitly
